dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6156 IH: Endorsement Transparency Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Onder (for himself and Mr. Harris of North Carolina) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Labor-Management Reporting and Disclosure Act of 1959 to require unions to poll their members prior to endorsing a presidential candidate.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Endorsement Transparency Act.

section.header: Requirement to poll members prior to presidential endorsement

subsection.text: Title I of the Labor-Management Reporting and Disclosure Act of 1959 (29 U.S.C. 411 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Requirement to endorse a presidential candidate
section.text: No labor organization may endorse a candidate in an election for the office of President of the United States unless the labor organization—

paragraph.text: polls the members of the labor organization with respect to such an endorsement; and
discloses the result of the poll described in paragraph (1) to the members of the labor organization.

subsection.text: The amendment made by this Act shall take effect on the date that is 12 months after the date of enactment of this Act.
